1→# Phase 06: Report, Presentation & Deployment
     2→
     3→**Owner:** Person C (report/slides), Person B (deployment) | **Days:** 6-10 | **Depends on:** Phase 05 (evaluation results) | **Status:** PENDING
     4→
     5→## Context
     6→- [Research 02 -- Academic framing](./research/researcher-02-evaluation-deployment.md#4-academic-framing)
     7→- [Research 02 -- ONNX browser deployment](./research/researcher-02-evaluation-deployment.md#2-onnx-export)
     8→- [Research -- Vision-first multi-modal legitimacy](../research-vision-first-multimodal.md)
     9→- Existing game: `/Users/hieudinh/Documents/my-projects/CVsubject/game/`
    10→
    11→## Overview
    12→
    13→Two parallel tracks: (1) Person C writes the academic report and prepares presentation slides using evaluation results from Phase 05; (2) Person B modifies `gesture.js` to load both MLP and CNN ONNX models, performing late fusion inference in the browser.
    14→
    15→## Key Insights
    16→- Report structure follows standard ML paper format (Research 02, Sec 4.4)
    17→- Title recommendation: "Camera-Based Multi-Modal Hand Gesture Recognition: Fusing Skeleton Landmarks and Visual Appearance"
    18→- Deployment uses separate ONNX models + JS weighted average (simplest, most maintainable)
    19→- CNN input in browser: capture hand crop from MediaPipe bbox, resize to 224x224, normalize with ImageNet stats
    20→- WebGPU for CNN acceleration; WASM fallback for Safari/Firefox
    21→
    22→## Requirements
    23→
    24→### Report
    25→- LaTeX or Word template
    26→- All figures/tables from Phase 05
    27→- Key references from research reports
    28→
    29→### Deployment
    30→- CNN ONNX model from Phase 03 (<10MB)
    31→- Existing MLP ONNX model (`game/mlp_model.onnx`)
    32→- `onnxruntime-web` already loaded via CDN in `index.html`
    33→
    34→## Architecture: Browser Deployment
    35→
    36→```
    37→Camera Frame
    38→    |
    39→    v
    40→MediaPipe Hands (existing)
    41→    |
    42→    +--> 21 landmarks --> normalize (existing) --> MLP ONNX --> pose_logits (5-dim)
    43→    |                                                              |
    44→    +--> Hand bbox --> crop from video frame --> resize 224x224     |
    45→         --> ImageNet normalize --> CNN ONNX --> appearance_logits  |
    46→                                                    |              |
    47→                                            alpha * pose + (1-alpha) * appearance
    48→                                                    |
    49→                                            argmax --> gesture label
    50→                                                    |
    51→                                            FrameDetector (if 2 hands) --> "frame" override
    52→```
    53→
    54→## Related Code Files
    55→- `/Users/hieudinh/Documents/my-projects/CVsubject/game/gesture.js` -- `GestureController` class; modify for dual-model inference
    56→- `/Users/hieudinh/Documents/my-projects/CVsubject/game/main.js` -- game loop; no changes needed
    57→- `/Users/hieudinh/Documents/my-projects/CVsubject/game/index.html` -- add CNN model path, ensure onnxruntime-web CDN
    58→- `/Users/hieudinh/Documents/my-projects/CVsubject/game/mlp_model.onnx` -- existing MLP model
    59→
    60→## Implementation Steps
    61→
    62→### Step 6a: Modify gesture.js for dual-model inference (Person B, Day 7-8)
    63→
    64→Key changes to `GestureController`:
    65→
    66→```javascript
    67→class GestureController {
    68→  static GESTURE_LABELS = ['fist', 'frame', 'none', 'open_hand', 'pinch'];
    69→
    70→  constructor(videoElement, onGestureChange) {
    71→    // ... existing fields ...
    72→    this.mlpSession = null;
    73→    this.cnnSession = null;
    74→    this.fusionAlpha = 0.7;  // MLP weight (tuned in Phase 04)
    75→    this._cropCanvas = document.createElement('canvas');
    76→    this._cropCanvas.width = 224;
    77→    this._cropCanvas.height = 224;
    78→    this._cropCtx = this._cropCanvas.getContext('2d');
    79→  }
    80→
    81→  async init() {
    82→    // ... existing MediaPipe init ...
    83→    this.mlpSession = await ort.InferenceSession.create('./mlp_model.onnx', {
    84→      executionProviders: ['wasm']
    85→    });
    86→    this.cnnSession = await ort.InferenceSession.create('./cnn_model.onnx', {
    87→      executionProviders: ['webgpu', 'wasm']
    88→    });
    89→  }
    90→
    91→  _extractHandCrop(landmarks, videoWidth, videoHeight) {
    92→    // Compute bounding box from landmarks
    93→    const xs = landmarks.map(l => l.x * videoWidth);
    94→    const ys = landmarks.map(l => l.y * videoHeight);
    95→    const minX = Math.min(...xs), maxX = Math.max(...xs);
    96→    const minY = Math.min(...ys), maxY = Math.max(...ys);
    97→    const pad = 0.1;
    98→    const w = maxX - minX, h = maxY - minY;
    99→    const x1 = Math.max(0, minX - pad * w);
   100→    const y1 = Math.max(0, minY - pad * h);
   101→    const x2 = Math.min(videoWidth, maxX + pad * w);
   102→    const y2 = Math.min(videoHeight, maxY + pad * h);
   103→
   104→    // Draw crop to offscreen canvas, resize to 224x224
   105→    this._cropCtx.drawImage(this.video, x1, y1, x2-x1, y2-y1, 0, 0, 224, 224);
   106→    const imageData = this._cropCtx.getImageData(0, 0, 224, 224);
   107→
   108→    // Convert to CHW float32 with ImageNet normalization
   109→    const mean = [0.485, 0.456, 0.406];
   110→    const std = [0.229, 0.224, 0.225];
   111→    const float32Data = new Float32Array(3 * 224 * 224);
   112→    for (let i = 0; i < 224 * 224; i++) {
   113→      for (let c = 0; c < 3; c++) {
   114→        float32Data[c * 224 * 224 + i] =
   115→          (imageData.data[i * 4 + c] / 255.0 - mean[c]) / std[c];
   116→      }
   117→    }
   118→    return new ort.Tensor('float32', float32Data, [1, 3, 224, 224]);
   119→  }
   120→
   121→  async _fusedPredict(landmarks, handLandmarks) {
   122→    // MLP inference (existing)
   123→    const mlpInput = this._normalizeLandmarks(handLandmarks);
   124→    const mlpTensor = new ort.Tensor('float32', mlpInput, [1, 60]);
   125→    const mlpResult = await this.mlpSession.run({ input: mlpTensor });
   126→    const mlpProbs = this._softmax(mlpResult.output.data);
   127→
   128→    // CNN inference
   129→    const cropTensor = this._extractHandCrop(
   130→      handLandmarks, this.video.videoWidth, this.video.videoHeight
   131→    );
   132→    const cnnResult = await this.cnnSession.run({ image: cropTensor });
   133→    const cnnProbs = this._softmax(cnnResult.logits.data);
   134→
   135→    // Late fusion: weighted average
   136→    const fused = mlpProbs.map((p, i) =>
   137→      this.fusionAlpha * p + (1 - this.fusionAlpha) * cnnProbs[i]
   138→    );
   139→    return fused;
   140→  }
   141→}
   142→```
   143→
   144→**Important**: Check ONNX input/output names match what was exported. Use `session.inputNames` and `session.outputNames` to verify.
   145→
   146→### Step 6b: Add fallback to MLP-only (Person B, Day 8)
   147→
   148→If CNN model fails to load (e.g., WebGPU unavailable), fall back to MLP-only inference:
   149→
   150→```javascript
   151→async init() {
   152→  this.mlpSession = await ort.InferenceSession.create('./mlp_model.onnx');
   153→  try {
   154→    this.cnnSession = await ort.InferenceSession.create('./cnn_model.onnx', {
   155→      executionProviders: ['webgpu', 'wasm']
   156→    });
   157→    console.log('CNN model loaded; fusion mode active');
   158→  } catch (e) {
   159→    console.warn('CNN model failed to load; MLP-only mode:', e);
   160→    this.cnnSession = null;
   161→  }
   162→}
   163→```
   164→
   165→### Step 6c: Report writing (Person C, Days 6-10)
   166→
   167→Report structure (from Research 02, Sec 4.4):
   168→
   169→```
   170→1. Introduction
   171→   - Problem: HGR for interactive applications
   172→   - Motivation: skeleton alone misses appearance cues; fusion improves robustness
   173→   - Contributions: (1) multi-modal fusion of skeleton+appearance, (2) person-aware evaluation,
   174→     (3) comparison with end-to-end detection (YOLO)
   175→
   176→2. Related Work
   177→   - 2.1 Skeleton-based HGR (MediaPipe, GCN)
   178→   - 2.2 Appearance-based HGR (CNN classifiers)
   179→   - 2.3 Multi-modal fusion (Zhu 2022, Cho 2026, Shu 2022)
   180→   - 2.4 Object detection for gestures (YOLO)
   181→
   182→3. Methodology
   183→   - 3.1 Dataset: HaGRID v2 (5 classes, 9,231 samples, 3,607 subjects)
   184→   - 3.2 MediaPipe preprocessing (landmark extraction, normalization)
   185→   - 3.3 Landmark classifier (MLP: 60->128->64->5)
   186→   - 3.4 Appearance classifier (MobileNetV3-Small on 224x224 crops)
   187→   - 3.5 Late fusion strategies (weighted avg, learned concat)
   188→   - 3.6 Inter-hand frame detection (post-processing)
   189→   - 3.7 YOLO detection baseline (YOLOv8n)
   190→
   191→4. Experimental Setup
   192→   - 4.1 Dataset statistics (Table: samples per class)
   193→   - 4.2 Person-aware Group 5-Fold CV
   194→   - 4.3 Metrics: accuracy, F1-macro, mAP (for YOLO)
   195→   - 4.4 Hardware & hyperparameters
   196→
   197→5. Results
   198→   - 5.1 Comparison table (all methods)
   199→   - 5.2 Ablation table (modality contributions)
   200→   - 5.3 Confusion matrices
   201→   - 5.4 Per-class F1 analysis
   202→   - 5.5 Detection vs classification comparison
   203→   - 5.6 Inference latency
   204→
   205→6. Discussion
   206→   - When does fusion help? (error analysis on confused classes)
   207→   - Why is skeleton so strong? (5 classes are geometrically distinct)
   208→   - Detection vs classification trade-offs
   209→   - Limitations (single camera, static gestures, 5 classes)
   210→
   211→7. Conclusion
   212→   - Summary of findings
   213→   - Browser deployment as practical contribution
   214→
   215→References
   216→```
   217→
   218→### Step 6d: Presentation slides (Person C, Days 9-10)
   219→
   220→Slide outline (~15 slides):
   221→1. Title + team
   222→2. Problem statement
   223→3. Dataset overview (HaGRID, 5 classes, sample images)
   224→4. System architecture diagram (pipeline figure)
   225→5. Method A: MLP on landmarks
   226→6. Method B: CNN on crops
   227→7. Method C: Multi-modal fusion
   228→8. Method D: YOLO detection
   229→9. Evaluation protocol (person-aware K-Fold)
   230→10. Results: comparison table
   231→11. Results: ablation table
   232→12. Results: confusion matrices
   233→13. Demo: browser game screenshot
   234→14. Discussion & limitations
   235→15. Conclusion & future work
   236→
   237→### Step 6e: Deploy to GitHub Pages (Person B, Day 9-10)
   238→
   239→Existing workflow at `.github/workflows/` handles Pages deployment. Add `cnn_model.onnx` to game directory:
   240→
   241→- Copy `game/cnn_model.onnx` (~5-10MB)
   242→- Ensure GitHub Pages serves `.onnx` files with correct MIME type
   243→- Test on Chrome (WebGPU) and Firefox (WASM fallback)
   244→
   245→## Todo
   246→
   247→- [ ] Modify `game/gesture.js` for dual-model inference
   248→- [ ] Add hand crop extraction from video frame
   249→- [ ] Add ImageNet normalization in JS
   250→- [ ] Add MLP-only fallback if CNN fails to load
   251→- [ ] Test browser inference with both models
   252→- [ ] Measure browser inference latency (target: <100ms per frame)
   253→- [ ] Write Introduction section
   254→- [ ] Write Related Work section
   255→- [ ] Write Methodology section
   256→- [ ] Write Experimental Setup section
   257→- [ ] Insert tables and figures from Phase 05
   258→- [ ] Write Results section
   259→- [ ] Write Discussion section
   260→- [ ] Write Conclusion
   261→- [ ] Create presentation slides
   262→- [ ] Deploy updated game to GitHub Pages
   263→- [ ] End-to-end test: play jigsaw puzzle with fusion model
   264→
   265→## Success Criteria
   266→- Browser demo works with fusion (MLP + CNN) in Chrome
   267→- Graceful fallback to MLP-only in browsers without WebGPU
   268→- Inference latency < 100ms per frame (real-time)
   269→- Report covers all 7 sections with tables/figures
   270→- Presentation has ~15 slides with pipeline diagram
   271→- GitHub Pages deployment live and functional
   272→
   273→## Risk Assessment
   274→
   275→| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
   276→|------|-------------|--------|------------|
   277→| CNN too slow in browser (>100ms) | Medium | Medium | Reduce input size to 128x128; use WebGPU; or drop to MLP-only for demo |
   278→| CNN ONNX model too large for Pages | Low | Low | MobileNetV3-Small ~5MB; acceptable |
   279→| CHW vs HWC tensor format mismatch | High | High | Verify PyTorch export is CHW; JS code must match |
   280→| Report not finished in time | Medium | High | Start writing early (Day 6); fill with placeholder data, update later |
   281→| WebGPU not available in demo environment | Medium | Medium | WASM fallback; mention in report as limitation |
   282→
   283→## Security Considerations
   284→- ONNX models served from GitHub Pages are public
   285→- No sensitive data in models (weights only)
   286→- CORS: same-origin policy satisfied if models served from same domain
   287→
   288→## Next Steps
   289→- This is the final phase. Deliverables: working demo, academic report, presentation slides
   290→- Optional stretch: deploy YOLO model for browser-side detection (requires significant ONNX optimization)
   291→

data.prompt:
You are implementing Phase 06 (browser deployment) of a vision-first multi-modal hand gesture recognition system. Your job is to modify `game/gesture.js` to support dual-model inference (MLP + CNN) with late fusion.

## What to read first
1. `/Users/hieudinh/Documents/my-projects/CVsubject/game/gesture.js` - existing GestureController (MUST read fully)
2. `/Users/hieudinh/Documents/my-projects/CVsubject/game/main.js` - game loop for context
3. `/Users/hieudinh/Documents/my-projects/CVsubject/game/index.html` - check onnxruntime-web CDN
4. `REDACTED.md` - detailed plan

## What to modify in `game/gesture.js`

Add dual-model fusion inference to the existing `GestureController` class:

### New properties
- `this.cnnSession = null` - ONNX session for CNN model
- `this.fusionAlpha = 0.7` - MLP weight for fusion (MLP dominant since 98% vs ~90%)
- `this._cropCanvas` - offscreen canvas for hand crop extraction (224x224)
- `this._cropCtx` - 2D context for crop canvas
- `this.fusionEnabled = false` - flag indicating if CNN loaded successfully

### New methods

1. **`_extractHandCrop(landmarks, videoWidth, videoHeight)`**
   - Compute bounding box from 21 landmarks (min/max x,y)
   - Add 10% padding
   - Draw cropped region to offscreen 224x224 canvas
   - Convert to CHW Float32Array with ImageNet normalization:
     - mean=[0.485, 0.456, 0.406], std=[0.229, 0.224, 0.225]
     - RGBA imageData -> CHW float32 (skip alpha channel)
   - Return `new ort.Tensor('float32', float32Data, [1, 3, 224, 224])`

2. **`_softmax(logits)`** (if not already present)
   - Numerically stable softmax on Float32Array

3. **`_fusedPredict(handLandmarks)`**
   - Run MLP inference (existing normalization -> ONNX)
   - Extract hand crop from video frame -> CNN ONNX inference
   - Apply softmax to both outputs
   - Weighted average: `alpha * mlp_probs + (1-alpha) * cnn_probs`
   - Return fused probabilities array

### Modified methods

4. **`init()`** - After existing MLP session creation, try loading CNN:
   ```javascript
   try {
     this.cnnSession = await ort.InferenceSession.create('./cnn_model.onnx', {
       executionProviders: ['webgpu', 'wasm']
     });
     this.fusionEnabled = true;
     console.log('CNN model loaded; fusion mode active');
   } catch (e) {
     console.warn('CNN model failed to load; MLP-only mode:', e.message);
     this.cnnSession = null;
     this.fusionEnabled = false;
   }
   ```

5. **Main prediction method** - If fusionEnabled, use `_fusedPredict()`, else fallback to existing MLP-only prediction.

### Critical details
- CNN ONNX input name: `"image"`, output name: `"logits"` (from Phase 03 export)
- MLP ONNX input/output names: check existing code
- CHW format (PyTorch convention): channel-first, not HWC
- ImageNet normalization: `(pixel/255 - mean) / std` per channel
- The video element is available as `this.video` or through the constructor
- IMPORTANT: Check existing code for how the video element and landmarks are accessed

## Important
- DO NOT break existing MLP-only functionality
- CNN is optional - graceful fallback
- Keep the existing API contract (same methods called by main.js)
- Minimize changes to existing code

WRITE the modified code. Edit the existing gesture.js file.

input.old_string:
/**
 * GestureController - MediaPipe HandLandmarker integration with
 * ONNX-based MLP gesture classification.
 *
 * Gestures detected (via trained MLP model):
 *   "pinch"     - Thumb tip close to index tip
 *   "fist"      - All fingers curled
 *   "open_hand" - All fingers extended
 *   "frame"     - Two hands forming a rectangle (bypass: both hands detected)
 *   "none"      - No hand or ambiguous pose
 */
input.new_string:
/**
 * GestureController - MediaPipe HandLandmarker integration with
 * ONNX-based MLP gesture classification and optional CNN fusion.
 *
 * When the CNN model is available, late fusion combines MLP (skeleton)
 * and CNN (appearance) predictions via weighted average. Falls back
 * to MLP-only inference when CNN fails to load.
 *
 * Gestures detected (via trained MLP model):
 *   "pinch"     - Thumb tip close to index tip
 *   "fist"      - All fingers curled
 *   "open_hand" - All fingers extended
 *   "frame"     - Two hands forming a rectangle (bypass: both hands detected)
 *   "none"      - No hand or ambiguous pose
 */

- `this.fusionAlpha = 0.7` - MLP weight for fusion (MLP dominant since 98% vs ~90%)
- `this._cropCanvas` - offscreen canvas for hand crop extraction (224x224)
- `this._cropCtx` - 2D context for crop canvas
- `this.fusionEnabled = false` - flag indicating if CNN loaded successfully

### New methods

   - Compute bounding box from 21 landmarks (min/max x,y)
   - Add 10% padding
   - Draw cropped region to offscreen 224x224 canvas
   - Convert to CHW Float32Array with ImageNet normalization:
     - mean=[0.485, 0.456, 0.406], std=[0.229, 0.224, 0.225]

   - Run MLP inference (existing normalization -> ONNX)
   - Extract hand crop from video frame -> CNN ONNX inference
   - Apply softmax to both outputs
   - Weighted average: `alpha * mlp_probs + (1-alpha) * cnn_probs`
   - Return fused probabilities array

content.text:
The implementation is complete. Here is a summary of all changes made to `/Users/hieudinh/Documents/my-projects/CVsubject/game/gesture.js`:

---

## Changes Summary

### 1. Updated file header (lines 1-15)
Added documentation about CNN fusion and MLP-only fallback behavior.

### 2. New constructor properties (lines 32-44)
```javascript
// ONNX inference sessions
this.session = null;       // MLP session
this.cnnSession = null;    // CNN session (optional)
this.fusionEnabled = false; // true when CNN loaded successfully
this.fusionAlpha = 0.7;    // MLP weight for fusion (MLP dominant: 98% vs ~90%)

// Offscreen canvas for hand crop extraction (CNN input)
this._cropCanvas = document.createElement('canvas');
this._cropCanvas.width = 224;
this._cropCanvas.height = 224;
this._cropCtx = this._cropCanvas.getContext('2d');
```

### 3. Modified `_initONNXSession()` (lines 163-188)
Now loads both MLP (required) and CNN (optional with graceful fallback). CNN tries `webgpu` first, then falls back to `wasm`. If CNN fails entirely, `fusionEnabled` stays `false` and the system continues with MLP-only mode.

### 4. Modified `_runInference()` (lines 288-325)
After running MLP inference, checks `this.fusionEnabled`. If true, calls `_fusedPredict()` to combine MLP and CNN outputs. Otherwise uses MLP output directly. The argmax/confidence logic at the end is unchanged.

### 5. New method `_fusedPredict(mlpOutput, landmarks)` (lines 333-350)
- Applies softmax to MLP output
- Extracts hand crop from video, runs CNN inference (input name: `"image"`, output name: `"logits"`)
- Applies softmax to CNN output
- Returns weighted average: `alpha * mlp_probs + (1 - alpha) * cnn_probs`

### 6. New method `_extractHandCrop(landmarks, videoWidth, videoHeight)` (lines 361-410)
- Computes bounding box from all 21 landmarks in pixel coordinates
- Adds 10% padding on each side, clamped to video dimensions
- Draws cropped region to offscreen 224x224 canvas via `drawImage`
- Converts RGBA `ImageData` to CHW `Float32Array` with ImageNet normalization (`mean=[0.485, 0.456, 0.406]`, `std=[0.229, 0.224, 0.225]`)
- Returns `ort.Tensor` of shape `[1, 3, 224, 224]`

### 7. New method `_softmax(logits)` (lines 417-429)
Numerically stable softmax (subtracts max before exponentiation).

### 8. Modified `destroy()` (lines 635-652)
Cleans up `cnnSession`, resets `fusionEnabled`, and nulls the crop canvas references.

### What was NOT changed
- All existing public API methods (`init()`, `initDetector()`, `detectRaw()`, `classifyLandmarksAsync()`, `getHandPosition()`, `getCurrentGesture()`, `getConfidence()`, `setOverlayCanvas()`, `destroy()`)
- `_processResults()` flow -- unchanged, still calls `_runInference()` the same way
- `_preprocessLandmarks()` -- unchanged
- `_classifyGesture()` heuristic fallback -- unchanged
- `_drawOverlay()` -- unchanged
- `main.js` requires zero modifications
- `index.html` requires no changes (onnxruntime-web CDN is already loaded)

### ONNX model contract
- MLP: input `"input"` shape `[1, 60]`, output `"output"` (probabilities)
- CNN: input `"image"` shape `[1, 3, 224, 224]`, output `"logits"` (raw logits, softmax applied in JS)
- The CNN model file `cnn_model.onnx` must be placed in the `game/` directory alongside `mlp_model.onnx`

content.content:
1→"""Download HaGRID v2 512px images from HuggingFace for target gesture classes.
     2→
     3→Downloads images for 5 target classes needed by our gesture recognition pipeline:
     4→  stop -> open_hand, fist -> fist, thumb_index -> pinch,
     5→  take_picture -> frame, no_gesture -> none
     6→
     7→Uses huggingface_hub.snapshot_download to pull only the relevant class folders
     8→from the Tinkoff/hagrid_v2_512 dataset (~3GB per class, ~15GB total).
     9→
    10→Usage:
    11→    python ml/scripts/download_hagrid.py
    12→    python ml/scripts/download_hagrid.py --save_dir data/hagrid_images --classes fist stop
    13→"""
    14→
    15→from __future__ import annotations
    16→
    17→import argparse
    18→import logging
    19→import sys
    20→from pathlib import Path
    21→
    22→logging.basicConfig(
    23→    level=logging.INFO,
    24→    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s",
    25→    datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
    26→)
    27→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    28→
    29→REPO_ID = "Tinkoff/hagrid_v2_512"
    30→TARGET_CLASSES = ["stop", "fist", "thumb_index", "take_picture", "no_gesture"]
    31→
    32→
    33→def download_class(
    34→    hagrid_class: str,
    35→    save_dir: str,
    36→    repo_id: str = REPO_ID,
    37→) -> Path:
    38→    """Download a single HaGRID gesture class from HuggingFace.
    39→
    40→    Args:
    41→        hagrid_class: HaGRID class name (e.g. "fist", "stop").
    42→        save_dir: Local directory to save downloaded images.
    43→        repo_id: HuggingFace dataset repo ID.
    44→
    45→    Returns:
    46→        Path to the local download directory.
    47→    """
    48→    try:
    49→        from huggingface_hub import snapshot_download
    50→    except ImportError:
    51→        logger.error(
    52→            "huggingface_hub is not installed. "
    53→            "Install it with: pip install huggingface_hub"
    54→        )
    55→        sys.exit(1)
    56→
    57→    logger.info("Downloading class '%s' from %s ...", hagrid_class, repo_id)
    58→
    59→    local_dir = snapshot_download(
    60→        repo_id=repo_id,
    61→        repo_type="dataset",
    62→        allow_patterns=[f"{hagrid_class}/*"],
    63→        local_dir=save_dir,
    64→    )
    65→
    66→    result_path = Path(local_dir) / hagrid_class
    67→    if result_path.is_dir():
    68→        count = sum(1 for f in result_path.iterdir() if f.is_file())
    69→        logger.info("  Downloaded %d files to %s", count, result_path)
    70→    else:
    71→        logger.warning("  Download completed but directory %s not found", result_path)
    72→
    73→    return result_path
    74→
    75→
    76→def download_all(
    77→    save_dir: str,
    78→    classes: list[str] | None = None,
    79→    repo_id: str = REPO_ID,
    80→) -> dict[str, int]:
    81→    """Download all target gesture classes.
    82→
    83→    Args:
    84→        save_dir: Root directory to save downloaded images.
    85→        classes: List of HaGRID class names to download. Defaults to TARGET_CLASSES.
    86→        repo_id: HuggingFace dataset repo ID.
    87→
    88→    Returns:
    89→        Dict mapping class name to number of downloaded files.
    90→    """
    91→    classes = classes or TARGET_CLASSES
    92→    save_path = Path(save_dir)
    93→    save_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    94→
    95→    invalid = [c for c in classes if c not in TARGET_CLASSES]
    96→    if invalid:
    97→        logger.warning(
    98→            "Classes not in target set (will download anyway): %s", invalid
    99→        )
   100→
   101→    stats: dict[str, int] = {}
   102→    for i, cls in enumerate(classes, 1):
   103→        logger.info("[%d/%d] Processing class: %s", i, len(classes), cls)
   104→        try:
   105→            result_path = download_class(cls, save_dir, repo_id)
   106→            if result_path.is_dir():
   107→                count = sum(1 for f in result_path.iterdir() if f.is_file())
   108→                stats[cls] = count
   109→            else:
   110→                stats[cls] = 0
   111→        except Exception:
   112→            logger.exception("Failed to download class '%s'", cls)
   113→            stats[cls] = -1
   114→
   115→    return stats
   116→
   117→
   118→def main() -> None:
   119→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   120→        description="Download HaGRID v2 512px images from HuggingFace.",
   121→        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
   122→        epilog="""\
   123→Examples:
   124→  # Download all 5 target classes
   125→  python ml/scripts/download_hagrid.py
   126→
   127→  # Download to custom directory
   128→  python ml/scripts/download_hagrid.py --save_dir /data/hagrid
   129→
   130→  # Download specific classes only
   131→  python ml/scripts/download_hagrid.py --classes fist stop
   132→
   133→  # Use a different repo (e.g. full-res)
   134→  python ml/scripts/download_hagrid.py --repo_id Tinkoff/hagrid_v2
   135→""",
   136→    )
   137→    parser.add_argument(
   138→        "--save_dir",
   139→        default="data/hagrid_images",
   140→        help="Directory to save downloaded images (default: data/hagrid_images)",
   141→    )
   142→    parser.add_argument(
   143→        "--classes",
   144→        nargs="+",
   145→        default=None,
   146→        choices=TARGET_CLASSES,
   147→        help=f"Classes to download (default: all {len(TARGET_CLASSES)} target classes)",
   148→    )
   149→    parser.add_argument(
   150→        "--repo_id",
   151→        default=REPO_ID,
   152→        help=f"HuggingFace dataset repo ID (default: {REPO_ID})",
   153→    )
   154→
   155→    args = parser.parse_args()
   156→
   157→    logger.info("Save directory: %s", args.save_dir)
   158→    logger.info("Target classes: %s", args.classes or TARGET_CLASSES)
   159→    logger.info("Repo: %s", args.repo_id)
   160→
   161→    stats = download_all(
   162→        save_dir=args.save_dir,
   163→        classes=args.classes,
   164→        repo_id=args.repo_id,
   165→    )
   166→
   167→    # Print summary
   168→    print(f"\n{'=' * 50}")
   169→    print("  Download Summary")
   170→    print(f"{'=' * 50}")
   171→    print(f"  {'Class':<16} {'Files':>8}")
   172→    print(f"  {'-' * 28}")
   173→    total = 0
   174→    for cls, count in sorted(stats.items()):
   175→        status = f"{count:>8}" if count >= 0 else "  FAILED"
   176→        print(f"  {cls:<16} {status}")
   177→        if count > 0:
   178→            total += count
   179→    print(f"  {'-' * 28}")
   180→    print(f"  {'TOTAL':<16} {total:>8}")
   181→    print(f"\n  Saved to: {args.save_dir}")
   182→
   183→    failed = [c for c, n in stats.items() if n < 0]
   184→    if failed:
   185→        logger.error("Failed classes: %s", failed)
   186→        sys.exit(1)
   187→
   188→
   189→if __name__ == "__main__":
   190→    main()
   191→

content.content:
1→"""Convert HaGRID v2 annotations to YOLO detection format.
     2→
     3→Reads HaGRID annotation JSONs and produces per-image .txt label files in
     4→YOLO format: ``class_id x_center y_center width height`` (all normalized 0-1).
     5→
     6→Handles:
     7→- COCO top-left bbox -> YOLO center bbox conversion
     8→- Multiple hands per image (multiple lines per txt)
     9→- Bbox clipping to [0, 1]
    10→- Person-aware train/val split via user_id
    11→- data.yaml generation for YOLO training
    12→
    13→HaGRID bbox format:  [x_tl, y_tl, w, h] normalized
    14→YOLO bbox format:    [x_center, y_center, w, h] normalized
    15→
    16→Class mapping (alphabetical for deterministic ordering):
    17→  fist=0, no_gesture=1, stop=2, take_picture=3, thumb_index=4
    18→
    19→Usage:
    20→    python ml/scripts/convert_to_yolo.py
    21→    python ml/scripts/convert_to_yolo.py --annotations_dir data/annotations/annotations \\
    22→        --images_dir data/hagrid_images --output_dir data/yolo --val_ratio 0.2
    23→"""
    24→
    25→from __future__ import annotations
    26→
    27→import argparse
    28→import json
    29→import logging
    30→import os
    31→import random
    32→import shutil
    33→import sys
    34→from pathlib import Path
    35→
    36→logging.basicConfig(
    37→    level=logging.INFO,
    38→    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s",
    39→    datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
    40→)
    41→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    42→
    43→# HaGRID classes we care about, mapped to YOLO class IDs (alphabetical)
    44→YOLO_CLASS_MAP: dict[str, int] = {
    45→    "fist": 0,
    46→    "no_gesture": 1,
    47→    "stop": 2,
    48→    "take_picture": 3,
    49→    "thumb_index": 4,
    50→}
    51→
    52→# Reverse map for data.yaml
    53→YOLO_CLASS_NAMES: dict[int, str] = {v: k for k, v in YOLO_CLASS_MAP.items()}
    54→
    55→# Our gesture names for reference
    56→HAGRID_TO_OURS: dict[str, str] = {
    57→    "fist": "fist",
    58→    "no_gesture": "none",
    59→    "stop": "open_hand",
    60→    "take_picture": "frame",
    61→    "thumb_index": "pinch",
    62→}
    63→
    64→TARGET_HAGRID_CLASSES = set(YOLO_CLASS_MAP.keys())
    65→
    66→
    67→def coco_to_yolo(x_tl: float, y_tl: float, w: float, h: float) -> tuple[float, float, float, float]:
    68→    """Convert COCO [x_tl, y_tl, w, h] to YOLO [x_center, y_center, w, h].
    69→
    70→    All values are normalized 0-1. The bbox is first clipped to the image
    71→    boundary [0, 1] in top-left/bottom-right space, then converted to YOLO
    72→    center format.
    73→
    74→    Args:
    75→        x_tl: Top-left x coordinate (normalized).
    76→        y_tl: Top-left y coordinate (normalized).
    77→        w: Width (normalized).
    78→        h: Height (normalized).
    79→
    80→    Returns:
    81→        Tuple of (x_center, y_center, width, height), all in [0, 1].
    82→    """
    83→    # Clip the COCO bbox to [0, 1] in pixel-boundary space
    84→    x1 = max(0.0, x_tl)
    85→    y1 = max(0.0, y_tl)
    86→    x2 = min(1.0, x_tl + w)
    87→    y2 = min(1.0, y_tl + h)
    88→
    89→    # Recompute width/height after clipping
    90→    w_clipped = max(0.0, x2 - x1)
    91→    h_clipped = max(0.0, y2 - y1)
    92→
    93→    # Convert to center format
    94→    x_center = x1 + w_clipped / 2.0
    95→    y_center = y1 + h_clipped / 2.0
    96→
    97→    return x_center, y_center, w_clipped, h_clipped
    98→
    99→
   100→def find_annotation_files(annotations_dir: str) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
   101→    """Find annotation JSON files organized by split (train/val/test).
   102→
   103→    Supports both split-organized and flat directory structures:
   104→        annotations_dir/train/*.json  OR  annotations_dir/*.json
   105→
   106→    Args:
   107→        annotations_dir: Root directory containing annotation JSONs.
   108→
   109→    Returns:
   110→        Dict mapping split name to list of JSON file paths.
   111→    """
   112→    ann_path = Path(annotations_dir)
   113→    result: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
   114→
   115→    for split in ["train", "val", "test"]:
   116→        split_dir = ann_path / split
   117→        if split_dir.is_dir():
   118→            jsons = sorted(split_dir.glob("*.json"))
   119→            target_jsons = [
   120→                str(j) for j in jsons if j.stem in TARGET_HAGRID_CLASSES
   121→            ]
   122→            if target_jsons:
   123→                result[split] = target_jsons
   124→
   125→    if not result:
   126→        jsons = sorted(ann_path.glob("*.json"))
   127→        target_jsons = [
   128→            str(j) for j in jsons if j.stem in TARGET_HAGRID_CLASSES
   129→        ]
   130→        if target_jsons:
   131→            result["all"] = target_jsons
   132→
   133→    return result
   134→
   135→
   136→def parse_annotations_for_yolo(
   137→    json_path: str,
   138→    hagrid_class: str,
   139→) -> dict[str, list[dict]]:
   140→    """Parse a single HaGRID annotation JSON into per-image YOLO entries.
   141→
   142→    Args:
   143→        json_path: Path to the annotation JSON file.
   144→        hagrid_class: HaGRID class name this file represents.
   145→
   146→    Returns:
   147→        Dict mapping image_id to list of hand annotation dicts, each with:
   148→            class_id, x_center, y_center, w, h, user_id
   149→    """
   150→    if hagrid_class not in YOLO_CLASS_MAP:
   151→        logger.warning("Skipping unknown class: %s", hagrid_class)
   152→        return {}
   153→
   154→    class_id = YOLO_CLASS_MAP[hagrid_class]
   155→
   156→    with open(json_path, "r") as f:
   157→        data = json.load(f)
   158→
   159→    result: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}
   160→    skipped = 0
   161→
   162→    for image_id, entry in data.items():
   163→        bboxes = entry.get("bboxes", [])
   164→        labels = entry.get("labels", [])
   165→        user_id = entry.get("user_id", "unknown")
   166→
   167→        if not bboxes:
   168→            skipped += 1
   169→            continue
   170→
   171→        hands: list[dict] = []
   172→        for hand_idx, bbox in enumerate(bboxes):
   173→            # Only include hands matching this gesture class
   174→            if hand_idx < len(labels) and labels[hand_idx] != hagrid_class:
   175→                continue
   176→
   177→            if len(bbox) != 4:
   178→                logger.warning(
   179→                    "Invalid bbox for image %s hand %d: %s",
   180→                    image_id, hand_idx, bbox,
   181→                )
   182→                continue
   183→
   184→            x_tl, y_tl, w, h = bbox
   185→
   186→            # Skip degenerate bboxes
   187→            if w <= 0 or h <= 0:
   188→                logger.warning(
   189→                    "Zero/negative bbox for image %s hand %d: w=%.4f h=%.4f",
   190→                    image_id, hand_idx, w, h,
   191→                )
   192→                continue
   193→
   194→            x_c, y_c, w_y, h_y = coco_to_yolo(x_tl, y_tl, w, h)
   195→
   196→            hands.append({
   197→                "class_id": class_id,
   198→                "x_center": x_c,
   199→                "y_center": y_c,
   200→                "w": w_y,
   201→                "h": h_y,
   202→                "user_id": user_id,
   203→            })
   204→
   205→        if hands:
   206→            if image_id in result:
   207→                result[image_id].extend(hands)
   208→            else:
   209→                result[image_id] = hands
   210→
   211→    if skipped:
   212→        logger.debug("  Skipped %d images with no bboxes", skipped)
   213→
   214→    return result
   215→
   216→
   217→def collect_user_ids(
   218→    all_annotations: dict[str, list[dict]],
   219→) -> dict[str, str]:
   220→    """Collect user_id for each image_id from parsed annotations.
   221→
   222→    Args:
   223→        all_annotations: Dict mapping image_id to list of hand annotation dicts.
   224→
   225→    Returns:
   226→        Dict mapping image_id to user_id.
   227→    """
   228→    image_user: dict[str, str] = {}
   229→    for image_id, hands in all_annotations.items():
   230→        if hands:
   231→            image_user[image_id] = hands[0]["user_id"]
   232→    return image_user
   233→
   234→
   235→def person_aware_split(
   236→    image_user: dict[str, str],
   237→    val_ratio: float = 0.2,
   238→    seed: int = 42,
   239→) -> tuple[set[str], set[str]]:
   240→    """Split image IDs into train/val sets ensuring no user leaks across sets.
   241→
   242→    Users (identified by user_id) are assigned entirely to either train or val.
   243→
   244→    Args:
   245→        image_user: Dict mapping image_id to user_id.
   246→        val_ratio: Fraction of users to assign to validation.
   247→        seed: Random seed for reproducibility.
   248→
   249→    Returns:
   250→        Tuple of (train_image_ids, val_image_ids).
   251→    """
   252→    # Group images by user
   253→    user_images: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
   254→    for image_id, user_id in image_user.items():
   255→        user_images.setdefault(user_id, []).append(image_id)
   256→
   257→    users = sorted(user_images.keys())
   258→    rng = random.Random(seed)
   259→    rng.shuffle(users)
   260→
   261→    n_val_users = max(1, int(len(users) * val_ratio))
   262→    val_users = set(users[:n_val_users])
   263→    train_users = set(users[n_val_users:])
   264→
   265→    train_ids: set[str] = set()
   266→    val_ids: set[str] = set()
   267→
   268→    for user_id in train_users:
   269→        train_ids.update(user_images[user_id])
   270→    for user_id in val_users:
   271→        val_ids.update(user_images[user_id])
   272→
   273→    logger.info(
   274→        "Person-aware split: %d users train, %d users val -> %d/%d images",
   275→        len(train_users), len(val_users), len(train_ids), len(val_ids),
   276→    )
   277→
   278→    return train_ids, val_ids
   279→
   280→
   281→def write_yolo_label(
   282→    label_path: Path,
   283→    hands: list[dict],
   284→) -> None:
   285→    """Write a YOLO label file for one image.
   286→
   287→    Args:
   288→        label_path: Output .txt file path.
   289→        hands: List of hand annotation dicts with class_id, x_center, y_center, w, h.
   290→    """
   291→    label_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   292→    with open(label_path, "w") as f:
   293→        for hand in hands:
   294→            line = (
   295→                f"{hand['class_id']} "
   296→                f"{hand['x_center']:.6f} "
   297→                f"{hand['y_center']:.6f} "
   298→                f"{hand['w']:.6f} "
   299→                f"{hand['h']:.6f}\n"
   300→            )
   301→            f.write(line)
   302→
   303→
   304→def find_image_file(images_dir: Path, image_id: str) -> Path | None:
   305→    """Locate the image file for a given image_id across class subdirectories.
   306→
   307→    HaGRID images are organized as: images_dir/{class}/{image_id}.jpg
   308→
   309→    Args:
   310→        images_dir: Root directory containing class subdirectories.
   311→        image_id: Image filename stem (without extension).
   312→
   313→    Returns:
   314→        Path to the image file, or None if not found.
   315→    """
   316→    for ext in [".jpg", ".jpeg", ".png"]:
   317→        # Search in class subdirectories
   318→        for class_dir in images_dir.iterdir():
   319→            if not class_dir.is_dir():
   320→                continue
   321→            candidate = class_dir / f"{image_id}{ext}"
   322→            if candidate.exists():
   323→                return candidate
   324→        # Also check flat structure
   325→        candidate = images_dir / f"{image_id}{ext}"
   326→        if candidate.exists():
   327→            return candidate
   328→    return None
   329→
   330→
   331→def copy_or_symlink_image(
   332→    src: Path,
   333→    dst: Path,
   334→    symlink: bool = False,
   335→) -> None:
   336→    """Copy or symlink an image to the YOLO output directory.
   337→
   338→    Args:
   339→        src: Source image path.
   340→        dst: Destination image path.
   341→        symlink: If True, create symlink instead of copying.
   342→    """
   343→    dst.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   344→    if dst.exists():
   345→        return
   346→    if symlink:
   347→        dst.symlink_to(src.resolve())
   348→    else:
   349→        shutil.copy2(src, dst)
   350→
   351→
   352→def write_data_yaml(
   353→    output_dir: Path,
   354→    train_dir: Path,
   355→    val_dir: Path,
   356→) -> Path:
   357→    """Generate data.yaml for YOLO training.
   358→
   359→    Args:
   360→        output_dir: Root output directory.
   361→        train_dir: Path to training images directory.
   362→        val_dir: Path to validation images directory.
   363→
   364→    Returns:
   365→        Path to the generated data.yaml file.
   366→    """
   367→    yaml_path = output_dir / "data.yaml"
   368→
   369→    # Use absolute paths for YOLO compatibility
   370→    content = (
   371→        f"path: {output_dir.resolve()}\n"
   372→        f"train: {train_dir.resolve()}\n"
   373→        f"val: {val_dir.resolve()}\n"
   374→        f"\n"
   375→        f"nc: {len(YOLO_CLASS_MAP)}\n"
   376→        f"names:\n"
   377→    )
   378→    for idx in sorted(YOLO_CLASS_NAMES.keys()):
   379→        content += f"  {idx}: {YOLO_CLASS_NAMES[idx]}\n"
   380→
   381→    with open(yaml_path, "w") as f:
   382→        f.write(content)
   383→
   384→    logger.info("Generated data.yaml at %s", yaml_path)
   385→    return yaml_path
   386→
   387→
   388→def convert_to_yolo(
   389→    annotations_dir: str,
   390→    images_dir: str,
   391→    output_dir: str,
   392→    val_ratio: float = 0.2,
   393→    split_seed: int = 42,
   394→    symlink: bool = False,
   395→    splits: list[str] | None = None,
   396→    max_per_class: int | None = None,
   397→) -> dict[str, dict[str, int]]:
   398→    """Convert HaGRID annotations to YOLO format with person-aware split.
   399→
   400→    Args:
   401→        annotations_dir: Directory containing HaGRID annotation JSONs.
   402→        images_dir: Directory containing HaGRID images.
   403→        output_dir: Output directory for YOLO dataset.
   404→        val_ratio: Fraction of users for validation split.
   405→        split_seed: Random seed for split reproducibility.
   406→        symlink: If True, symlink images instead of copying.
   407→        splits: Which annotation splits to process (None = all).
   408→        max_per_class: Maximum images per class (None = all).
   409→
   410→    Returns:
   411→        Nested dict: {split: {class: count}}.
   412→    """
   413→    split_files = find_annotation_files(annotations_dir)
   414→    if not split_files:
   415→        logger.error("No target annotation files found in %s", annotations_dir)
   416→        sys.exit(1)
   417→
   418→    logger.info("Found annotation splits: %s", list(split_files.keys()))
   419→
   420→    if splits:
   421→        split_files = {s: f for s, f in split_files.items() if s in splits}
   422→
   423→    # Phase 1: Parse all annotations
   424→    all_annotations: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}
   425→    class_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
   426→
   427→    for split_name, json_files in split_files.items():
   428→        logger.info("Parsing annotations from split: %s", split_name)
   429→        for json_path in json_files:
   430→            hagrid_class = Path(json_path).stem
   431→            if hagrid_class not in TARGET_HAGRID_CLASSES:
   432→                continue
   433→
   434→            logger.info("  Parsing %s ...", hagrid_class)
   435→            parsed = parse_annotations_for_yolo(json_path, hagrid_class)
   436→
   437→            # Merge into global dict (image may appear in multiple class files
   438→            # if it has hands of different gesture types)
   439→            for image_id, hands in parsed.items():
   440→                if image_id in all_annotations:
   441→                    all_annotations[image_id].extend(hands)
   442→                else:
   443→                    all_annotations[image_id] = hands
   444→
   445→            class_counts[hagrid_class] = class_counts.get(hagrid_class, 0) + len(parsed)
   446→            logger.info("    %d images with %s hands", len(parsed), hagrid_class)
   447→
   448→    logger.info("Total images with annotations: %d", len(all_annotations))
   449→
   450→    if max_per_class:
   451→        logger.info("Limiting to %d images per class", max_per_class)
   452→
   453→    # Phase 2: Person-aware split
   454→    image_user = collect_user_ids(all_annotations)
   455→    train_ids, val_ids = person_aware_split(image_user, val_ratio, split_seed)
   456→
   457→    # Phase 3: Write YOLO labels and organize images
   458→    output_path = Path(output_dir)
   459→    images_path = Path(images_dir)
   460→
   461→    train_img_dir = output_path / "train" / "images"
   462→    train_lbl_dir = output_path / "train" / "labels"
   463→    val_img_dir = output_path / "val" / "images"
   464→    val_lbl_dir = output_path / "val" / "labels"
   465→
   466→    for d in [train_img_dir, train_lbl_dir, val_img_dir, val_lbl_dir]:
   467→        d.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   468→
   469→    stats: dict[str, dict[str, int]] = {"train": {}, "val": {}}
   470→    missing_images = 0
   471→
   472→    for image_id, hands in all_annotations.items():
   473→        # Determine split
   474→        if image_id in val_ids:
   475→            split = "val"
   476→            img_dir = val_img_dir
   477→            lbl_dir = val_lbl_dir
   478→        elif image_id in train_ids:
   479→            split = "train"
   480→            img_dir = train_img_dir
   481→            lbl_dir = train_lbl_dir
   482→        else:
   483→            # Shouldn't happen, but default to train
   484→            split = "train"
   485→            img_dir = train_img_dir
   486→            lbl_dir = train_lbl_dir
   487→
   488→        # Find source image
   489→        src_image = find_image_file(images_path, image_id)
   490→        if src_image is None:
   491→            missing_images += 1
   492→            continue
   493→
   494→        # Write label file
   495→        label_file = lbl_dir / f"{image_id}.txt"
   496→        write_yolo_label(label_file, hands)
   497→
   498→        # Copy/symlink image
   499→        dst_image = img_dir / src_image.name
   500→        copy_or_symlink_image(src_image, dst_image, symlink)
   501→
   502→        # Track stats
   503→        for hand in hands:
   504→            cls_name = YOLO_CLASS_NAMES[hand["class_id"]]
   505→            stats[split][cls_name] = stats[split].get(cls_name, 0) + 1
   506→
   507→    if missing_images:
   508→        logger.warning(
   509→            "%d images referenced in annotations but not found in %s",
   510→            missing_images, images_dir,
   511→        )
   512→
   513→    # Phase 4: Generate data.yaml
   514→    write_data_yaml(output_path, train_img_dir, val_img_dir)
   515→
   516→    return stats
   517→
   518→
   519→def main() -> None:
   520→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   521→        description="Convert HaGRID annotations to YOLO detection format.",
   522→        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
   523→        epilog="""\
   524→Examples:
   525→  # Convert with default settings
   526→  python ml/scripts/convert_to_yolo.py
   527→
   528→  # Custom directories and val ratio
   529→  python ml/scripts/convert_to_yolo.py \\
   530→      --annotations_dir data/annotations/annotations \\
   531→      --images_dir data/hagrid_images \\
   532→      --output_dir data/yolo \\
   533→      --val_ratio 0.2
   534→
   535→  # Use symlinks instead of copying images (saves disk space)
   536→  python ml/scripts/convert_to_yolo.py --symlink
   537→
   538→  # Only process train split annotations
   539→  python ml/scripts/convert_to_yolo.py --splits train
   540→""",
   541→    )
   542→    parser.add_argument(
   543→        "--annotations_dir",
   544→        default="data/annotations/annotations",
   545→        help="HaGRID annotations directory (default: data/annotations/annotations)",
   546→    )
   547→    parser.add_argument(
   548→        "--images_dir",
   549→        default="data/hagrid_images",
   550→        help="HaGRID images directory (default: data/hagrid_images)",
   551→    )
   552→    parser.add_argument(
   553→        "--output_dir",
   554→        default="data/yolo",
   555→        help="Output directory for YOLO dataset (default: data/yolo)",
   556→    )
   557→    parser.add_argument(
   558→        "--val_ratio",
   559→        type=float,
   560→        default=0.2,
   561→        help="Fraction of users for validation split (default: 0.2)",
   562→    )
   563→    parser.add_argument(
   564→        "--split_seed",
   565→        type=int,
   566→        default=42,
   567→        help="Random seed for person-aware split (default: 42)",
   568→    )
   569→    parser.add_argument(
   570→        "--symlink",
   571→        action="store_true",
   572→        help="Symlink images instead of copying (saves disk space)",
   573→    )
   574→    parser.add_argument(
   575→        "--splits",
   576→        nargs="+",
   577→        default=None,
   578→        help="Which annotation splits to process (default: all). E.g., --splits train",
   579→    )
   580→    parser.add_argument(
   581→        "--max_per_class",
   582→        type=int,
   583→        default=None,
   584→        help="Maximum images per class (default: all)",
   585→    )
   586→
   587→    args = parser.parse_args()
   588→
   589→    logger.info("Annotations dir: %s", args.annotations_dir)
   590→    logger.info("Images dir:      %s", args.images_dir)
   591→    logger.info("Output dir:      %s", args.output_dir)
   592→    logger.info("Val ratio:       %.2f", args.val_ratio)
   593→    logger.info("Split seed:      %d", args.split_seed)
   594→    logger.info("Symlink mode:    %s", args.symlink)
   595→
   596→    stats = convert_to_yolo(
   597→        annotations_dir=args.annotations_dir,
   598→        images_dir=args.images_dir,
   599→        output_dir=args.output_dir,
   600→        val_ratio=args.val_ratio,
   601→        split_seed=args.split_seed,
   602→        symlink=args.symlink,
   603→        splits=args.splits,
   604→        max_per_class=args.max_per_class,
   605→    )
   606→
   607→    # Print summary
   608→    print(f"\n{'=' * 60}")
   609→    print("  YOLO Conversion Summary")
   610→    print(f"{'=' * 60}")
   611→
   612→    for split_name in ["train", "val"]:
   613→        split_stats = stats.get(split_name, {})
   614→        total = sum(split_stats.values())
   615→        print(f"\n  [{split_name.upper()}] ({total} hand annotations)")
   616→        print(f"  {'Class':<16} {'Hands':>8}")
   617→        print(f"  {'-' * 28}")
   618→        for cls in sorted(split_stats.keys()):
   619→            print(f"  {cls:<16} {split_stats[cls]:>8}")
   620→
   621→    print(f"\n  Output directory: {args.output_dir}")
   622→    print(f"  data.yaml:       {args.output_dir}/data.yaml")
   623→
   624→    # Verify output
   625→    output_path = Path(args.output_dir)
   626→    for split in ["train", "val"]:
   627→        img_count = len(list((output_path / split / "images").glob("*")))
   628→        lbl_count = len(list((output_path / split / "labels").glob("*.txt")))
   629→        print(f"  {split}: {img_count} images, {lbl_count} labels")
   630→        if img_count != lbl_count:
   631→            logger.warning(
   632→                "%s: image count (%d) != label count (%d)",
   633→                split, img_count, lbl_count,
   634→            )
   635→

   638→    main()

content.content:
1→"""Extract hand crops from HaGRID images using bbox annotations.
     2→
     3→Reads HaGRID annotation JSONs and extracts hand region crops from the
     4→corresponding images. Crops are padded by 10%, resized to 224x224, and
     5→organized by class for CNN classifier training.
     6→
     7→Output structure:
     8→    data/crops/{class}/{image_id}_{hand_idx}.jpg
     9→    data/crop_metadata.csv
    10→
    11→The metadata CSV contains columns:
    12→    image_id, hand_idx, class, user_id, crop_path
    13→
    14→Usage:
    15→    python ml/scripts/extract_crops.py
    16→    python ml/scripts/extract_crops.py --annotations_dir data/annotations/annotations \\
    17→        --images_dir data/hagrid_images --output_dir data/crops --crop_size 224
    18→"""
    19→
    20→from __future__ import annotations
    21→
    22→import argparse
    23→import csv
    24→import json
    25→import logging
    26→import sys
    27→from pathlib import Path
    28→
    29→logging.basicConfig(
    30→    level=logging.INFO,
    31→    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s",
    32→    datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
    33→)
    34→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    35→
    36→# HaGRID -> our gesture class mapping (reused from convert_hagrid.py)
    37→GESTURE_MAP: dict[str, str] = {
    38→    "stop": "open_hand",
    39→    "fist": "fist",
    40→    "thumb_index": "pinch",
    41→    "take_picture": "frame",
    42→    "no_gesture": "none",
    43→}
    44→
    45→TARGET_HAGRID_CLASSES = set(GESTURE_MAP.keys())
    46→
    47→METADATA_HEADER = ["image_id", "hand_idx", "class", "user_id", "crop_path"]
    48→
    49→
    50→def extract_crop(
    51→    image_path: str | Path,
    52→    bbox: list[float],
    53→    output_path: str | Path,
    54→    crop_size: int = 224,
    55→    padding: float = 0.1,
    56→    jpeg_quality: int = 95,
    57→) -> bool:
    58→    """Crop hand region from image with padding, resize to target size.
    59→
    60→    Args:
    61→        image_path: Path to the source image.
    62→        bbox: HaGRID bbox [x_tl, y_tl, w, h] normalized 0-1.
    63→        output_path: Path to save the cropped image.
    64→        crop_size: Target crop dimension (square).
    65→        padding: Fractional padding around bbox (0.1 = 10%).
    66→        jpeg_quality: JPEG save quality (1-100).
    67→
    68→    Returns:
    69→        True if crop was successfully extracted, False otherwise.
    70→    """
    71→    try:
    72→        from PIL import Image
    73→    except ImportError:
    74→        logger.error("Pillow is not installed. Install it with: pip install Pillow")
    75→        sys.exit(1)
    76→
    77→    try:
    78→        img = Image.open(image_path)
    79→    except Exception:
    80→        logger.warning("Cannot open image: %s", image_path)
    81→        return False
    82→
    83→    img_w, img_h = img.size
    84→    x_tl, y_tl, w, h = bbox
    85→
    86→    # Calculate padded crop coordinates in pixel space
    87→    pad_x = padding * w
    88→    pad_y = padding * h
    89→
    90→    x1 = max(0, int((x_tl - pad_x) * img_w))
    91→    y1 = max(0, int((y_tl - pad_y) * img_h))
    92→    x2 = min(img_w, int((x_tl + w + pad_x) * img_w))
    93→    y2 = min(img_h, int((y_tl + h + pad_y) * img_h))
    94→
    95→    # Validate crop region
    96→    if x2 <= x1 or y2 <= y1:
    97→        logger.warning(
    98→            "Degenerate crop region for %s: (%d,%d)-(%d,%d)",
    99→            image_path, x1, y1, x2, y2,
   100→        )
   101→        return False
   102→
   103→    crop_width = x2 - x1
   104→    crop_height = y2 - y1
   105→    if crop_width < 10 or crop_height < 10:
   106→        logger.warning(
   107→            "Crop too small (%dx%d) for %s", crop_width, crop_height, image_path
   108→        )
   109→        return False
   110→
   111→    try:
   112→        # Use LANCZOS for high-quality downsampling
   113→        from PIL import Image as PILImage
   114→
   115→        crop = img.crop((x1, y1, x2, y2))
   116→        crop = crop.resize((crop_size, crop_size), PILImage.LANCZOS)
   117→
   118→        output = Path(output_path)
   119→        output.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   120→
   121→        # Convert to RGB if necessary (handles RGBA, grayscale, etc.)
   122→        if crop.mode != "RGB":
   123→            crop = crop.convert("RGB")
   124→
   125→        crop.save(str(output), "JPEG", quality=jpeg_quality)
   126→        return True
   127→
   128→    except Exception:
   129→        logger.exception("Failed to extract crop from %s", image_path)
   130→        return False
   131→
   132→
   133→def find_image_file(images_dir: Path, image_id: str) -> Path | None:
   134→    """Locate the image file for a given image_id.
   135→
   136→    Searches class subdirectories and flat structure for common extensions.
   137→
   138→    Args:
   139→        images_dir: Root directory containing images.
   140→        image_id: Image filename stem (without extension).
   141→
   142→    Returns:
   143→        Path to the image file, or None if not found.
   144→    """
   145→    for ext in [".jpg", ".jpeg", ".png"]:
   146→        # Search in class subdirectories
   147→        for class_dir in images_dir.iterdir():
   148→            if not class_dir.is_dir():
   149→                continue
   150→            candidate = class_dir / f"{image_id}{ext}"
   151→            if candidate.exists():
   152→                return candidate
   153→        # Flat structure
   154→        candidate = images_dir / f"{image_id}{ext}"
   155→        if candidate.exists():
   156→            return candidate
   157→    return None
   158→
   159→
   160→def find_annotation_files(annotations_dir: str) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
   161→    """Find target annotation JSON files organized by split.
   162→
   163→    Args:
   164→        annotations_dir: Root directory containing annotation JSONs.
   165→
   166→    Returns:
   167→        Dict mapping split name to list of JSON file paths.
   168→    """
   169→    ann_path = Path(annotations_dir)
   170→    result: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
   171→
   172→    for split in ["train", "val", "test"]:
   173→        split_dir = ann_path / split
   174→        if split_dir.is_dir():
   175→            jsons = sorted(split_dir.glob("*.json"))
   176→            target_jsons = [
   177→                str(j) for j in jsons if j.stem in TARGET_HAGRID_CLASSES
   178→            ]
   179→            if target_jsons:
   180→                result[split] = target_jsons
   181→
   182→    if not result:
   183→        jsons = sorted(ann_path.glob("*.json"))
   184→        target_jsons = [
   185→            str(j) for j in jsons if j.stem in TARGET_HAGRID_CLASSES
   186→        ]
   187→        if target_jsons:
   188→            result["all"] = target_jsons
   189→
   190→    return result
   191→
   192→
   193→def extract_crops_from_annotations(
   194→    annotations_dir: str,
   195→    images_dir: str,
   196→    output_dir: str,
   197→    metadata_csv: str,
   198→    crop_size: int = 224,
   199→    padding: float = 0.1,
   200→    jpeg_quality: int = 95,
   201→    splits: list[str] | None = None,
   202→    max_per_class: int | None = None,
   203→) -> dict[str, int]:
   204→    """Extract hand crops from HaGRID images for all target classes.
   205→
   206→    Args:
   207→        annotations_dir: Directory containing HaGRID annotation JSONs.
   208→        images_dir: Directory containing HaGRID images.
   209→        output_dir: Output directory for crops, organized as {class}/{file}.jpg.
   210→        metadata_csv: Path to output metadata CSV.
   211→        crop_size: Target crop dimension (square).
   212→        padding: Fractional padding around bbox.
   213→        jpeg_quality: JPEG save quality.
   214→        splits: Which annotation splits to process (None = all).
   215→        max_per_class: Maximum crops per class (None = all).
   216→
   217→    Returns:
   218→        Dict mapping class name to number of crops extracted.
   219→    """
   220→    split_files = find_annotation_files(annotations_dir)
   221→    if not split_files:
   222→        logger.error("No target annotation files found in %s", annotations_dir)
   223→        sys.exit(1)
   224→
   225→    logger.info("Found annotation splits: %s", list(split_files.keys()))
   226→
   227→    if splits:
   228→        split_files = {s: f for s, f in split_files.items() if s in splits}
   229→
   230→    output_path = Path(output_dir)
   231→    output_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   232→    images_path = Path(images_dir)
   233→
   234→    # Prepare metadata CSV
   235→    metadata_path = Path(metadata_csv)
   236→    metadata_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   237→
   238→    metadata_rows: list[list[str]] = []
   239→    stats: dict[str, int] = {}
   240→    missing_images = 0
   241→    failed_crops = 0
   242→
   243→    for split_name, json_files in split_files.items():
   244→        logger.info("Processing annotations from split: %s", split_name)
   245→
   246→        for json_path in json_files:
   247→            hagrid_class = Path(json_path).stem
   248→            if hagrid_class not in TARGET_HAGRID_CLASSES:
   249→                continue
   250→
   251→            our_class = GESTURE_MAP[hagrid_class]
   252→            logger.info("  %s -> %s", hagrid_class, our_class)
   253→
   254→            with open(json_path, "r") as f:
   255→                data = json.load(f)
   256→
   257→            class_count = 0
   258→
   259→            for image_id, entry in data.items():
   260→                if max_per_class and class_count >= max_per_class:
   261→                    break
   262→
   263→                bboxes = entry.get("bboxes", [])
   264→                labels = entry.get("labels", [])
   265→                user_id = entry.get("user_id", "unknown")
   266→
   267→                if not bboxes:
   268→                    continue
   269→
   270→                # Find the source image
   271→                src_image = find_image_file(images_path, image_id)
   272→                if src_image is None:
   273→                    missing_images += 1
   274→                    continue
   275→
   276→                for hand_idx, bbox in enumerate(bboxes):
   277→                    # Only include hands matching this gesture class
   278→                    if hand_idx < len(labels) and labels[hand_idx] != hagrid_class:
   279→                        continue
   280→
   281→                    if len(bbox) != 4:
   282→                        logger.warning(
   283→                            "Invalid bbox for %s hand %d: %s",
   284→                            image_id, hand_idx, bbox,
   285→                        )
   286→                        continue
   287→
   288→                    # Skip degenerate bboxes
   289→                    if bbox[2] <= 0 or bbox[3] <= 0:
   290→                        continue
   291→
   292→                    if max_per_class and class_count >= max_per_class:
   293→                        break
   294→
   295→                    # Build output path: output_dir/{class}/{image_id}_{hand_idx}.jpg
   296→                    crop_filename = f"{image_id}_{hand_idx}.jpg"
   297→                    crop_rel_path = f"{our_class}/{crop_filename}"
   298→                    crop_abs_path = output_path / crop_rel_path
   299→
   300→                    success = extract_crop(
   301→                        image_path=src_image,
   302→                        bbox=bbox,
   303→                        output_path=crop_abs_path,
   304→                        crop_size=crop_size,
   305→                        padding=padding,
   306→                        jpeg_quality=jpeg_quality,
   307→                    )
   308→
   309→                    if success:
   310→                        class_count += 1
   311→                        # Store path relative to project root for portability
   312→                        crop_csv_path = str(Path(output_dir) / crop_rel_path)
   313→                        metadata_rows.append([
   314→                            image_id,
   315→                            str(hand_idx),
   316→                            our_class,
   317→                            user_id,
   318→                            crop_csv_path,
   319→                        ])
   320→                    else:
   321→                        failed_crops += 1
   322→
   323→            stats[our_class] = stats.get(our_class, 0) + class_count
   324→            logger.info("    Extracted %d crops", class_count)
   325→
   326→    # Write metadata CSV
   327→    with open(metadata_path, "w", newline="") as f:
   328→        writer = csv.writer(f)
   329→        writer.writerow(METADATA_HEADER)
   330→        writer.writerows(metadata_rows)
   331→
   332→    logger.info("Metadata CSV written to %s (%d rows)", metadata_path, len(metadata_rows))
   333→
   334→    if missing_images:
   335→        logger.warning("%d images not found in %s", missing_images, images_dir)
   336→    if failed_crops:
   337→        logger.warning("%d crops failed to extract", failed_crops)
   338→
   339→    return stats
   340→
   341→
   342→def main() -> None:
   343→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   344→        description="Extract hand crops from HaGRID images for CNN training.",
   345→        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
   346→        epilog="""\
   347→Examples:
   348→  # Extract all crops with default settings
   349→  python ml/scripts/extract_crops.py
   350→
   351→  # Custom directories
   352→  python ml/scripts/extract_crops.py \\
   353→      --annotations_dir data/annotations/annotations \\
   354→      --images_dir data/hagrid_images \\
   355→      --output_dir data/crops
   356→
   357→  # Limit to 500 crops per class (for quick experiments)
   358→  python ml/scripts/extract_crops.py --max_per_class 500
   359→
   360→  # Custom crop size and padding
   361→  python ml/scripts/extract_crops.py --crop_size 128 --padding 0.15
   362→""",
   363→    )
   364→    parser.add_argument(
   365→        "--annotations_dir",
   366→        default="data/annotations/annotations",
   367→        help="HaGRID annotations directory (default: data/annotations/annotations)",
   368→    )
   369→    parser.add_argument(
   370→        "--images_dir",
   371→        default="data/hagrid_images",
   372→        help="HaGRID images directory (default: data/hagrid_images)",
   373→    )
   374→    parser.add_argument(
   375→        "--output_dir",
   376→        default="data/crops",
   377→        help="Output directory for crops (default: data/crops)",
   378→    )
   379→    parser.add_argument(
   380→        "--metadata_csv",
   381→        default="data/crop_metadata.csv",
   382→        help="Output metadata CSV path (default: data/crop_metadata.csv)",
   383→    )
   384→    parser.add_argument(
   385→        "--crop_size",
   386→        type=int,
   387→        default=224,
   388→        help="Target crop size in pixels (default: 224)",
   389→    )
   390→    parser.add_argument(
   391→        "--padding",
   392→        type=float,
   393→        default=0.1,
   394→        help="Fractional padding around bbox (default: 0.1 = 10%%)",
   395→    )
   396→    parser.add_argument(
   397→        "--jpeg_quality",
   398→        type=int,
   399→        default=95,
   400→        help="JPEG save quality 1-100 (default: 95)",
   401→    )
   402→    parser.add_argument(
   403→        "--splits",
   404→        nargs="+",
   405→        default=None,
   406→        help="Which annotation splits to process (default: all). E.g., --splits train",
   407→    )
   408→    parser.add_argument(
   409→        "--max_per_class",
   410→        type=int,
   411→        default=None,
   412→        help="Maximum crops per class (default: all)",
   413→    )
   414→
   415→    args = parser.parse_args()
   416→
   417→    logger.info("Annotations dir: %s", args.annotations_dir)
   418→    logger.info("Images dir:      %s", args.images_dir)
   419→    logger.info("Output dir:      %s", args.output_dir)
   420→    logger.info("Metadata CSV:    %s", args.metadata_csv)
   421→    logger.info("Crop size:       %dx%d", args.crop_size, args.crop_size)
   422→    logger.info("Padding:         %.1f%%", args.padding * 100)
   423→
   424→    stats = extract_crops_from_annotations(
   425→        annotations_dir=args.annotations_dir,
   426→        images_dir=args.images_dir,
   427→        output_dir=args.output_dir,
   428→        metadata_csv=args.metadata_csv,
   429→        crop_size=args.crop_size,
   430→        padding=args.padding,
   431→        jpeg_quality=args.jpeg_quality,
   432→        splits=args.splits,
   433→        max_per_class=args.max_per_class,
   434→    )
   435→
   436→    # Print summary
   437→    print(f"\n{'=' * 50}")
   438→    print("  Crop Extraction Summary")
   439→    print(f"{'=' * 50}")
   440→    print(f"  {'Class':<16} {'Crops':>8}")
   441→    print(f"  {'-' * 28}")
   442→    total = 0
   443→    for cls in sorted(stats.keys()):
   444→        print(f"  {cls:<16} {stats[cls]:>8}")
   445→        total += stats[cls]
   446→    print(f"  {'-' * 28}")
   447→    print(f"  {'TOTAL':<16} {total:>8}")
   448→    print(f"\n  Crops saved to:  {args.output_dir}")
   449→    print(f"  Metadata CSV:    {args.metadata_csv}")
   450→
   451→    # Verify output
   452→    output_path = Path(args.output_dir)
   453→    for cls_dir in sorted(output_path.iterdir()):
   454→        if cls_dir.is_dir():
   455→            count = sum(1 for f in cls_dir.iterdir() if f.is_file())
   456→            print(f"  {cls_dir.name}/: {count} files")
   457→
   458→
   459→if __name__ == "__main__":
   460→    main()
   461→
